channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Steam adds handy gifting and wishlist updates
item.description: You can now gift games without a Steam account and sort your wishlist into categories.

item.title: Google justifies its massive AI spending with a booming cloud business
item.description: Google's cloud business is thriving, as companies adopting its AI and AI infrastructure services help the tech giant to report record profits.

item.title: I’ve been gaming with 8BitDo's Xbox mobile controller grip and I’ve never used my Game Pass subscription more
item.description: The 8BitDo Ultimate Mobile Gaming Controller - Xbox Edition nails the cloud gaming experience but falls short in a few basic areas

item.title: Did you know you can use an AirPod to make an 'over-engineered frisbee stat tracker' that calculates spin and speed? No, of course you didn't — but someone's done it, and it works really well
item.description: Paul Tracer has created a smart Frisbee that records performance via an attached AirPod

item.title: 'There is no reason for any individual to have a computer in his home': Quote of the day by Digital Equipment Corporation co-founder Ken Olsen on the future of smart homes
item.description: The technology executive from yesteryear issues a seemingly stunning miscalculation regarding the proliferation of consumer-grade computing devices

item.title: Why Lettuce Is Always Making People Sick
item.description: The cyclospora diarrhea outbreak isn’t an isolated incident. It’s part of a pattern of leafy greens wreaking digestive havoc.

item.title: $50,000 T800 robot decapitates fellow android in world's first robotics MMA fight
item.description: A $50,000 T800 robot lost its head during Shenzhen's first humanoid robot fighting tournament, but continued fighting using its remaining systems.

item.title: The White House Is Trying to Figure Out What to Do About Chinese AI
item.description: There’s a debate going on in the Trump administration over how to handle increasingly powerful Chinese AI models.

item.title: Exclusive: It wasn't at this year's Unpacked, but a top Samsung exec hints that a new Galaxy Ring 'will definitely form part of our future portfolio of wearables'
item.description: Annika Bizon, VP of Product and Marketing for Samsung Mobile Experience, told us — well, very little, but she all but confirmed that anther Galaxy Ring is on the way.

item.title: US outlines its $5 billion Genesis Mission to boost science with AI
item.description: Several federal agencies are contributing toward the interdisciplinary research efforts.

item.title: Treasury threatens sanctions after White House claims Moonshot distilled Anthropic’s Fable
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent warned the U.S. government could sanction Chinese AI companies after White House officials accused Moonshot of distilling Anthropic's Fable model to develop Kimi K3.

item.title: Tesla spending skyrockets as Cybercab, Semi, Megapack production timeline slips
item.description: Tesla's 26% boost in revenue wasn't enough to offset rising operating expenses and capital expenditures as it pushes to launch a new generation of products.

item.title: Top Red-Light Therapy Deals at Nordstrom’s Anniversary Sale (2026)
item.description: Save on WIRED-tested LED therapy devices from TheraFace, HigherDose, and more.

item.title: Social media addiction lawsuit against Meta is dropped
item.description: A closely watched social media addiction lawsuit that had been set to go to trial next week has been dropped after the plaintiff voluntarily dismissed his claims against Meta, leaving none of the major tech companies facing trial in the case.

item.title: Andruil unveils autonomous attack rotorcraft - could Thunder be the future of warfare as we know it?
item.description: Anduril's new Thunder autonomous attack helicopter is built to fly as an escort for the Apache.

item.title: This $2 low-tech upgrade just made Russia's cheap 'cardboard-and-plywood' drone almost unjammable
item.description: Russia has reportedly added magnetic compasses to cheap Molniya drones, helping maintain direction when electronic warfare disrupts GPS and GLONASS signals.

item.title: Dépendance aux médias sociaux | Un adolescent renonce à un procès contre Meta
item.description: Un adolescent a renoncé au procès très attendu qui devait l’opposer, à partir de lundi à Los Angeles, à Meta, accusé d’avoir contribué à ses troubles psychologiques, ont indiqué mercredi ses avocats à l’AFP.

item.title: Tesla’s revenues are bouncing back, but profits are still weak
item.description: After a dismal two years of weakening demand, falling sales, and damage to its brand by Elon Musk's political activities, Tesla's road to recovery continues apace. On the heels of an impressive delivery report, the company released its earnings for the second quarter of 2026 - giving us the latest glimpse at the EV company […]

item.title: Nobody knows how bad corporate AI emissions really are. This startup has a way to estimate them
item.description: Investors, auditors, and regulators are asking companies about their AI emissions, but precisely measuring them is a challenge. More and more companies are implementing artificial intelligence into both their internal workflows and their external products, and that AI use comes with an environmental impact. Already, AI data centers are driving a surge in electricity demand that is outpacing supply....
